hook: You already know that $1 + 2 + 3 + \cdots + 100 = 5050$ — Gauss figured that out as a child. But contest problems rarely hand you a clean arithmetic sum; they twist sequences with recursion, cancellation, and hidden structure designed to slow you down. What if you had a toolkit that could crack almost any sum in seconds?
concept: Every topic in this unit is about transforming a complicated sum or sequence into something simple by exploiting hidden patterns — the unifying idea is that *structure, not brute force*, unlocks sequences, captured formally in the study of closed forms and generating functions.

topic_roadmap.description: Master contest-level shortcuts for arithmetic and geometric sums, including weighted sums and the 'multiply-and-subtract' technique that turns tricky series into one-line answers.
Learn to rewrite a sum so that consecutive terms cancel in a cascade, leaving only the first and last pieces and making even intimidating-looking series trivially easy to evaluate.
You will learn to solve recurrences by finding closed-form expressions, recognizing characteristic roots, and spotting the patterns that contest problems use to disguise recursive sequences.

Apply the powerful technique of encoding an entire sequence as the coefficients of a formal power series, then use algebraic manipulation to extract sums and closed forms that resist every other method.
